manual_1_1: This game finds mines differently from classic Minesweeper.
manual_1_2: Classic shows the mine count in the surrounding 8 cells, but
manual_1_3:
here, lasers fire in 8 directions (up/down/left/right/diagonals)
and show the mine count of tiles they touch.
manual_2_1:
Click an open tile to display its lasers
and see which tiles it connects to.
manual_3_1:
The mine count updates in real-time
as tiles are opened.
manual_3_2:
Use this to figure out which direction
a mine was found and identify it.
manual_3_3:
1. Select a specific opened tile.
2. Open a tile along one laser direction.
3. Repeat — stop when the selected tile's number increases.
manual_3_4: If the number increased, it means a new mine was found in that direction, so the tile you just opened has a mine.
manual_4_1: Mines aren't only found through lasers.
manual_4_2:
1. Open a tile.
2. If a number increases in one direction, check the opposite.
Opening tiles extends other tiles' lasers too.

You may find extra mines this way,
so watch which numbers change when you open a tile.
